---
title: "monte"
---

`monte` is a Python package designed for quick Monte Carlo modelling, with an emphasis
on distribution elicitation and composable models.

The intention is to make Monte Carlo models ergonomic enough for "back of the envelope"
problems in quick scripts or notebooks, while also supporting more advanced methods for
heavier simulation workloads.

`monte` is in development, and currently supports univariate distribution sampling,
including correlated sampling using Copulas.

This documentation site collects runnable examples and report-style walkthroughs.

## Examples

- [Working with Distributions](examples/distributions.qmd): the shared distribution API
for eliciting, fitting, plotting, and sampling.
- [Composing Monte Carlo Models with Distribution
Arithmetic](examples/mc-composition.qmd): a new product launch profit model built from
elicited distributions and ordinary Python arithmetic.
- [Correlated Sampling with Copulas](examples/correlated-sampling.qmd): the same product
launch model with correlated input sampling.
